subject: Re: Particle series export for a single tilt
body:
Thanks Alister
@Yunjie - just a note of caution - relion 5 does not support processing of
single tilt images in tomo mode… unfortunately, making a starfile
compatible with single particle averaging routines is not trivial… so
unfortunately we will need to wait until either warptools natively
generates spa type starfiles or relion5 tomo supports processing of single
projection starfiles

Best,
Pranav
--
Pranav Shah
Postdoctoral Research Fellow.

Division of Structural Biology,
Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
University of Oxford,
Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
UK


On Thu, 26 Jun 2025 at 21:07, Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com> wrote:

> After some wrangling of the build system 2.0.0dev34 is now available :-)
> happy processing!
>
> On Thu, 26 Jun 2025 at 10:14, Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com> wrote:
>
>> Hi Yunjie,
>>
>> Pranav actually submitted a PR with his fix for this, we just hadn't
>> pushed a release including it yet.
>>
>> I just pushed v2.0.0dev34, it will be available once this build is
>> finished
>> https://github.com/warpem/warp/actions/runs/15908175481/job/44868507261
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Alister
>>
>> On Thu, 26 Jun 2025 at 08:43, Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi Yunjie,
>>>
>>> C# code is compiled not interpreted, you will need to modify the source
>>> and build new binaries using the provided build scripts
>>>
>>> Cheers,
>>>
>>> Alister
>>>
>>> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>>>
>>> On Jun 26, 2025, at 07:41, Yunjie Chang <changyj0821@gmail.com> wrote:
>>>
>>> ﻿Dear Warp list,
>>>
>>> I’m currently using Warp v2.0.0dev33 and trying to export particle
>>> series using only the zero-tilt images from my dataset. However, I
>>> encountered the following error message during the process:
>>> *********************
>>>
>>> Exception details: System.ArgumentOutOfRangeException: Index was out of
>>> range. Must be non-negative and less than the size of the collection.
>>> (Parameter 'index')
>>>

>>> WarpTools.Commands.ExportParticlesTiltseries.Construct2DTomogramStarGeneralTable(TiltSeries

>>> opticsGroup) in

>>> 932
>>>
>>>    at

>>> 342

>>> 69
>>>
>>>    at
>>> WarpTools.Commands.BaseCommand.<>c__DisplayClass2_0`1.<IterateOverItems>g__ProcessItem|2(Int32
>>> index, Int32 threadID, Int32 jsonIndex) in
>>> /usr/share/miniconda/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1741217580690/work/WarpTools/Commands/BaseCommand.cs:line
>>> 191
>>> *********************
>>>
>>> Upon searching for similar issues, I noticed that about two months ago,
>>> Pranav commented on this GitHub comment (
>>> https://github.com/warpem/warp/pull/366), suggesting that modifying the
>>> ExportParticlesTiltseries.cs file could resolve the problem.
>>>
>>> I’d like to try replacing the ExportParticlesTiltseries.cs script on my
>>> workstation with the updated version. However, I’m unable to locate this
>>> file on my system. Could you please advise where this file is located or
>>> how I should proceed to apply the fix?
>>> Thanks!
>>> Yunjie
>>>
